title: Three nested learning loops — task, coordination, meta
summary: Appendix B.1.3's Adaptive Coordination Pattern made visible. Three reinforcing loops stack: task models learn from outcomes (R1), the coordination function learns which combinations work (R2), and a meta-coordination process learns how to improve coordination learning itself (R3). Each outer loop operates on a slower timescale and corrects the strategy of the inner loop.
source: Appendix B.1.3 — Adaptive Coordination Pattern (Three-Body Learning)

nodes.label: Task Models
nodes.description: Individual models A and B learning their own tasks

nodes.label: Coordination Function
nodes.description: C(A, B, Θ) — the learnable composition of task outputs

nodes.label: Meta Coordination
nodes.description: Learns which coordination strategies outperform others across episodes

nodes.label: Coordination Quality
nodes.description: Outcome quality — CQ, emergence index, user acceptance

identifiability_note: Each loop is observable via separate metrics: task accuracy, coordination quality (CQ), and meta-strategy change rate. Confounding between loops is controllable by fixing outer-loop parameters while inner-loop learning runs.
